lead: Deadline day: Iranian oil wind-down expires while the AI trade sells its best earnings

sources.use: Exact Q2 2026 results: net income NT$706.56B (+77.4% YoY), revenue NT$1,270.38B (+36.0% YoY), diluted EPS NT$27.25, gross margin 67.7%, operating margin 60.3%, net margin 55.6%.
Filed copy of the same Q2 results and guidance; backstop for every TSMC number used on air.
OFAC replaced General License X with narrower X1 after IRGC attacks on three tankers; no new Iranian oil sales, in-process transactions wind down until July 17.
Corroboration of the waiver revocation trigger (Hormuz tanker attacks) and the July 17 wind-down deadline; performance-based MOU framing.
Attributed report that Hormuz transits collapsed as fighting resumed; CENTCOM naval blockade reinstated July 14 per follow-on reporting. Battlefield casualty claims excluded.
July 16 session framing only: Dow -0.2%, S&P 500 -0.5%, Nasdaq -1.5%; chips down a second day; Alphabet -4%+ on reported Gemini 3.5 Pro delay (attributed to Bloomberg).

sources.publisher: U.S. Census Bureau (advance retail sales, June 2026)
sources.use: June advance retail sales +0.2% m/m versus +0.3% consensus. On-air phrasing keeps it one line; number must match the Census table before script approval.

exclusions: No casualty counts, no battlefield claims, no unconfirmed escalation options (WSJ 'options briefed' story excluded as single-source anonymous). Price levels come exclusively from TradingView capture, never from articles.
